Data Sources & Cadences

IES Solutions Design Team
Intelligence Hub
A centralized platform for real-time market intelligence, competitive analysis, training resources, and decision support — built to make the IES team faster and sharper on every pursuit.
Command Center
Live KPIs, top market alerts from competitor moves, tariff changes, and account signals — all in one view with interactive charts.
LIVE
Competitive & Account Intel
Track competitor news from DHL SC, Ryder, XPO, and CEVA. Monitor account signals and strategic shifts in real time.
LIVE
Market Intelligence Map
Interactive map of 10 key markets with labor data, real estate rates, and local news — click any pin for instant context.
LIVE
Training Wiki
Reference guides on warehouse robotics, conveyor systems, and engineering fundamentals — built for the team, by the team.
LIVE
Design Tools
Interactive warehouse sizing calculator with dynamic facility layout, zone allocation modeling, and IES engineering recommendations.
LIVE
Project Management
Full pipeline tracking with kanban views, forecast vs. actual hours, task management, and win strategy development per opportunity.
LIVE
Change Management
Track team changes through the ADKAR methodology. Manage activities, build process flowcharts, and auto-advance initiatives as work completes.
LIVE
Cost Model Builder
Build detailed operational cost models with MOST-driven labor, equipment, real estate, overhead, pricing buckets, and multi-year P&L projections.
BETA
Product Roadmap
Phase 1 — MVP
Live Now
  • Command Center with live KPIs
  • Competitive & Account Intel feeds
  • Market Intelligence Map (10 markets)
  • Top Market Alerts (multi-source)
  • Warehouse Sizing Calculator
  • Center of Gravity (COG)
  • Network Optimization
  • Training Wiki (23+ pages, 12 SCCs)
  • Industry Vertical Spotlights
  • Automation & Tech Intel
  • Project Management (Supabase)
  • Change Management (ADKAR)
Phase 2
Integrations & Analytics
  • Salesforce CRM connector
  • Snowflake data warehouse
  • Macro & Trade live data feeds
  • Labor Intelligence section
  • Win/loss analytics
Phase 3
Advanced Tools
  • Network tool: TL/LTL/parcel rates
  • Network tool: inbound flows & constraints
  • Cost Model Builder BETA
  • MOST work measurement tool
Phase 4
Platform & Scale
  • Financial modeling training
  • Live freight rate API integration
  • Scenario save & compare
  • Role-based access & permissions

OSHA / Regulatory Watch
OSHA finalizes heat illness prevention standard
Effective July 2026. Requires mandatory rest breaks when heat index exceeds 80°F. Direct impact on non-climate-controlled warehouses.
DOL proposes updated overtime threshold — $55,068
Would reclassify ~15% of warehouse supervisors. Model impact on client proposals for management-heavy operations.

AMR / Robotics Vendor Watch
WEEKLY
SYMBOTICMar 27, 2026
Symbotic launches "SymBot 2.0" — 40% faster throughput, new palletizing module
Direct relevance for grocery and F&B verticals. GXO has 6 active Symbotic deployments.

Locus Robotics raises $150M Series F; expands European operations

Geek+ partners with Shopify for integrated fulfillment robotics

6 River Systems rebrands under Ocado Intelligent Automation umbrella

Tariff Developments Tracker
DAILY
NEWMar 26, 2026
USTR announces 15% tariff on Chinese electronics — effective May 1, 2026
Covers consumer electronics, components, and smart home devices. Expected to accelerate nearshoring in this category. Model impact: +$0.12–$0.18/unit on affected SKUs.
TARIFFHIGH IMPACT
ACTIVEOngoing
Section 301 tariffs on Chinese goods — 25% on $250B tranche remains in effect
USTR review pending. Exclusion process narrowing. Key verticals affected: industrial machinery, auto parts, consumer goods.
TARIFF
WATCHMar 20, 2026
EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) Phase 2 — reporting obligations expand
Impacts imports of steel, cement, aluminum, fertilizers, hydrogen. Affects BTS costing for European-origin automation components.
REGULATORY

Win / Loss Pattern Analysis
DOS
TOP WIN FACTORS (correlation with wins)
1. Automation / tech differentiation in proposal — +18% win rate
2. Incumbent 3PL dissatisfaction (identified pre-RFP) — +14% win rate
3. Site visit within first 30 days — +11% win rate
4. Labor market with score >60 — +9% win rate
TOP LOSS FACTORS (correlation with losses)
1. Cycle time >120 days — -22% close rate
2. No GXO presence in target market — -16% close rate
3. Price-only evaluation criteria — -13% close rate
4. Healthcare vertical w/o compliance cert — -11% close rate

🔗 Salesforce Integration — Phase 2: These patterns will auto-update from CRM win/loss data with configurable time windows and vertical filters.

Industry Vertical Spotlights
Rotating deep-dives on sector-specific logistics trends and opportunities
Food & Beverage — Key Logistics Trends
Cold chain automation investment surging — +28% YoY in automated cold storage capacity
Temperature-controlled fulfillment is the fastest-growing automation segment. Key drivers: labor scarcity in cold environments, food safety regulations, and e-grocery growth.
FSMA Rule 204 enforcement begins — full traceability required for high-risk foods
Impacts: leafy greens, fresh-cut fruits, soft cheeses, nut butters, and more. WMS traceability capabilities become a must-have in proposals.
DTC food brands driving demand for small-parcel cold fulfillment
Market growing at 19% CAGR. Opportunity: multi-client cold fulfillment hubs serving DTC brands as a shared-services model.
F&B — GXO Differentiators
Temperature-controlled automation expertise across 50+ cold chain sites
GXO operates one of the largest 3PL cold chain networks in North America. Deep experience with freezer/cooler automation, including AS/RS and AMR in sub-zero environments.
FSMA/FDA compliance built into WMS configuration templates
Pre-configured lot tracking, expiration management, and FIFO enforcement. Reduces implementation timeline by 30% for food-grade facilities.
Multi-client cold fulfillment hub model — proven shared-services approach
Allows smaller DTC food brands to access enterprise-grade cold chain infrastructure at fractional cost. Strong margin profile for GXO.
Automotive — Key Logistics Trends
EV supply chain build-out creating massive parts logistics demand
Battery module sequencing, EV-specific aftermarket parts, and high-voltage component handling require specialized 3PL capabilities.
OEMs moving from JIT to "Just in Case" — buffer inventory strategy shift
Post-pandemic resilience push. OEMs increasing safety stock by 30-50%. Driving demand for additional warehousing capacity near assembly plants.
Automotive — GXO Differentiators
EV battery sequencing & high-voltage component handling capabilities
Specialized training programs and facility certifications for lithium-ion storage, HAZMAT compliance, and just-in-sequence delivery to assembly lines.
Aftermarket parts fulfillment network with 99.5%+ same-day ship rate
Multi-client parts distribution across 12 sites. Advanced slotting and wave optimization tuned for high-SKU, low-volume automotive aftermarket profiles.
Retail / E-commerce — Key Logistics Trends
Same-day delivery expectation hitting 3PL fulfillment design
Urban micro-fulfillment centers and forward-deploy inventory models becoming standard in RFP requirements.
Returns processing automation — "reverse logistics as a service" emerging
Returns cost retailers $890B annually. Automated returns processing reduces per-unit cost by 40%. High-margin service opportunity.
Retail — GXO Differentiators
Micro-fulfillment center (MFC) design expertise for same-day delivery
Proven urban MFC models with automated pick systems in under 30K SF footprints. Enables retailers to hit 2-hour delivery windows without massive capital outlay.
Returns processing automation — 40% cost reduction per unit
Proprietary returns triage workflow combining computer vision grading, automated re-packaging, and real-time inventory reintegration. High-margin service add-on.
Omnichannel fulfillment orchestration across DC + store networks
Unified inventory visibility and order routing across distribution centers and retail locations. Reduces split shipments by 35% and improves margin per order.

Platform Recommendation: Custom web app (Next.js + Supabase) for maximum flexibility. API aggregation layer handles all external feeds. Salesforce and Snowflake connect via API integrations. React front end with role-based views (Designer vs. Leadership). Estimated build: 8–12 weeks for MVP.

Volumes & Order Profile

Volume Lines

Define named volume lines that can be linked to labor activities. Each line auto-calculates daily volume based on operating days.

Process Area Name UOM Annual Volume Daily Volume Action
Order Profile
Total lines / total orders
Total units / total lines
%
Calculated Metrics
Total Orders per Year 0
Total Picks per Year 0
Seasonality Profile

Monthly volume multipliers relative to average. 1.0 = average month, 1.5 = 50% above average (e.g., holiday peak). Used for peak staffing and cash flow timing.

Peak: 1.0x | Avg: 1.0x

Labor Cost Adjustments

These defaults come from the selected market. Override per model as needed.

%
% of total hours paid at 1.5x rate (hourly roles)
%
FICA, health, 401k, workers comp, etc.
%
Annual bonus for salaried/indirect roles
Learning Curve / Ramp-Up

Ramp duration by complexity tier. Productivity starts at 60% and ramps to 100% over the defined period. Affects Year 1 labor costs.

Productivity curve: Week 1 = 60% → midpoint = 80% → end of ramp = 95% → steady state = 100%
Financial Metric Thresholds

Define target thresholds. Metrics below target show red; at or above show green.

Multi-Year Contract Budget

Annual operating P&L by contract year with volume growth and cost escalation.

Financial Metrics

Start-Up & Capital Costs

One-time costs amortized over the contract term. Auto-generated from equipment (racking, build-out, IT infrastructure) and implementation estimates.

Design Aid: Implementation Timeline

Pricing Buckets & Schedule

Define Pricing Buckets

Define how costs are grouped for customer pricing. Each cost line item across all sections can be assigned to a bucket.

Bucket Name Pricing Type UOM Volume Driver Annual Volume Action
Pricing Schedule

Aggregated costs by bucket. Fixed buckets show a monthly fee; variable buckets show a per-unit rate.

Bucket Annual Cost % of Total Rate / Fee UOM With Margin
TOTAL $0 100%
Unassigned Cost Items

Cost lines not yet assigned to a pricing bucket. Assign them in each section's table or they will default to Management Fee.

Based on pricing bucket assignments. Fixed = Management Fee bucket. Variable = all other buckets divided by their volume drivers.

Auto-generated guidance based on your inputs. Flags highlight parameters outside typical 3PL ranges.
